title: 10 best herbal teas for ringworm
intro_desc: Herbal teas for ringworm are infusions made from plants that have antifungal properties, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of ringworm, a common skin infection caused by fungi. Examples of herbal teas that can be beneficial for ringworm include chamomile, calendula, and echinacea. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe itchy and irritated skin, while calendula has antifungal and antiseptic properties that can help to kill the fungus causing the infection. Echinacea, on the other hand, can help to boost the immune system, which can speed up the healing process. By drinking these herbal teas, individuals with ringworm can experience relief from symptoms such as itching, redness, and inflammation, allowing them to go about their daily activities more comfortably.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tea helps with ringworm because it contains compounds like thujone and isothujone, which have antifungal properties. These compounds can inhibit the growth of the fungi that cause ringworm, helping to alleviate symptoms such as itching, redness, and skin irritation. Wormwood tea can be applied topically to the affected area or consumed as a beverage to help boost the immune system and speed up the healing process. However, it's important to note that wormwood tea should not be used as a sole treatment for ringworm and should be used in conjunction with medical treatment prescribed by a healthcare professional.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Wormwood tea contains compounds such as thujone and isothujone, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for ringworm, making it an effective natural remedy for treating the infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing.
Wound healing properties: Wormwood tea promotes wound healing by increasing blood flow to the affected area, allowing the body to quickly repair damaged tissue and recover from the infection.

Immune-boosting properties: Wormwood tea contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to stimulate the immune system, helping the body fight off the ringworm-causing fungus and preventing the spread of the infection.
Antipruritic properties: Wormwood tea has antipruritic properties, which can help alleviate the itching caused by ringworm, preventing scratching that can lead to further skin damage and infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Wormwood tea made from the leaves has antifungal properties that can help treat ringworm.
Flowers: The flowers of wormwood contain compounds that can help soothe skin irritation and reduce inflammation caused by ringworm.
Seeds: Wormwood seeds are rich in antioxidants that can help boost the immune system and promote healing in ringworm infections.
Stems: The stems of wormwood contain compounds that have antimicrobial properties, making them useful for treating ringworm.
remedy_recipe: In a mortar, crush 1 tablespoon of dried wormwood herb (Artemisia absinthium) to release its oils.
Transfer the crushed wormwood to a tea infuser and place it in a cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the wormwood in the infuser and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the infuser and discard the wormwood.
Drink the warm wormwood tea up to three times a day, between meals, for no more than two weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with ringworm because it contains anti-fungal and anti-microbial properties that can help to combat the fungus causing the infection. The active compounds in pot marigold, such as flavonoids and essential oils, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and promote the healing of affected skin. Furthermore, pot marigold tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with ringworm. Drinking pot marigold tea or applying it topically can help to alleviate the symptoms of ringworm and speed up the healing process.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Pot marigold tea contains antifungal compounds, such as flavonoids and essential oils, that help to combat the fungal infection causing ringworm.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory effects of pot marigold, due to compounds like quercetin and luteolin, help alleviate redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm.
Wound-healing properties: Pot marigold contains active constituents like triterpenes and saponins, which promote wound healing and may help damaged skin recover from ringworm infection.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of pot marigold tea, attributed to compounds like thujone and camphor, can help prevent secondary bacterial infections that may occur alongside ringworm.

Immune-boosting properties: Drinking pot marigold tea can help strengthen the immune system, thanks to its antioxidant properties, enabling the body to better fight off and recover from ringworm infections.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in antifungal properties, making them effective in treating ringworm infections.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant have potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, aiding in the healing process of ringworm-infected skin.
Stems: Although less potent than the flowers and leaves, the stems of the pot marigold plant still contain antifungal properties, making them useful in medicinal tea for ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 dried marigold flowers and place them in a tea infuser or teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the marigold flowers.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the medicinal compounds.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to help treat ringworm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with ringworm because it contains high levels of antioxidants and catechins, which have antifungal and antibacterial properties. These compounds can help to inhibit the growth and spread of the fungus that causes ringworm, known as Trichophyton. Additionally, white tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe the itching and irritation associated with ringworm. Drinking white tea or applying it topically as a compress or tea bag can provide relief from ringworm symptoms and promote healing. However, it is important to note that white tea should be used in conjunction with medical treatment prescribed by a healthcare professional for effective and safe treatment of ringworm.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: White tea contains antifungal compounds, such as catechins, which can help to inhibit the growth of the fungus that causes ringworm.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of white tea, due to its high flavonoid content, can help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm.

Antioxidant properties: White tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help to protect the skin from damage and support the immune system, contributing to the body's ability to fight off ringworm infections.
Antimicrobial properties: White tea exhibits antimicrobial properties, which can help to eliminate the fungus causing ringworm and prevent its spread.
Wound healing properties: White tea has been found to promote wound healing, which can help the skin recover from the infection and reduce the likelihood of scarring caused by ringworm.
remedy_parts: Leaves: White tea leaves are rich in antioxidants and have antifungal properties, making them effective in treating ringworm.
Buds: White tea buds contain high levels of catechins, a type of antioxidant that helps to inhibit the growth of fungi causing ringworm.
Stems: The stems of the white tea plant have been found to have antifungal properties, making them useful in the preparation of medicinal tea for ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and add 2 teaspoons of dried herbal white tea leaves, simmer for 3-5 minutes.
Remove tea from heat, add 1 teaspoon of crushed calendula flowers, steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea, discarding the solids, let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Soak a clean cloth in the tea, squeeze out excess liquid.
Apply the cloth to the affected area for 15-20 minutes, repeat 3 times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with ringworm because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate the symptoms of ringworm. The tea's active compounds, such as chamazulene and bisabolol, have been shown to have antifungal effects, which can help to inhibit the growth of the fungus that causes ringworm. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm. Drinking the tea or applying it topically as a compress can provide relief and promote healing of the affected area. However, it is important to note that while German chamomile tea can help with ringworm, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially for severe or persistent cases.
remedy_properties: Anti-fungal: German chamomile tea contains active constituents such as chamazulene and bisabolol, which have been shown to have anti-fungal properties, making it effective in treating ringworm by inhibiting the growth of the fungus that causes the infection.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of German chamomile tea, due to the presence of flavonoids and other compounds, can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, providing relief to those affected.

Antioxidant: German chamomile tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help boost the immune system and promote healing, aiding in the recovery from ringworm by supporting the body's natural defense mechanisms.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of German chamomile tea, attributed to its essential oils and other compounds, can help prevent the spread of ringworm by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria and fungi, promoting a healthier skin environment.

Wound healing: German chamomile tea has been shown to have wound healing properties, due to the presence of compounds like alpha-bisabolol, which can help speed up the healing process and reduce the appearance of scars, making it helpful in treating ringworm and promoting skin regeneration.
remedy_parts: Flowers: German chamomile tea made from its daisy-like flowers is often used as a topical treatment for skin conditions like ringworm due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties.
Leaves: The leaves of German chamomile contain similar medicinal compounds as its flowers, and they can be used interchangeably to make tea for treating ringworm.

Seeds: The seeds of German chamomile contain essential oils that exhibit antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for treating skin conditions like ringworm when used in tea form.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2-3 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers and place them in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil water and pour it over the chamomile flowers, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the medicinal properties.
Strain the tea to remove the chamomile flowers, creating a clear liquid.
Drink the herbal tea, up to three times a day, to help treat ringworm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory tea helps with ringworm because it contains anti-fungal properties that can help to combat the infection causing the skin condition. The main active compound in chicory, called chicoric acid, has been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm. Additionally, chicory tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help to boost the immune system and promote overall skin health. Drinking chicory tea or applying it topically as a compress may help to speed up the healing process and alleviate symptoms of ringworm. However, it is important to note that chicory tea should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment and should be used in conjunction with any prescribed medication.
remedy_properties: Fungicidal properties: Chicory tea has been found to have fungicidal properties, which are important for ringworm as the condition is caused by a type of fungus. The active constituents that give chicory its fungicidal properties include tannins and saponins.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Chicory tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm. The active constituents that contribute to chicory's anti-inflammatory properties include sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic acids.

Antioxidant properties: Chicory tea has strong antioxidant properties, which can help protect the skin from damage and promote healing. The active constituents that contribute to chicory's antioxidant properties include polyphenols, flavonoids, and caffeic acid.

Immune-boosting properties: Chicory tea has immune-boosting properties, which can help the body fight off the fungal infection causing ringworm. The active constituents that contribute to chicory's immune-boosting properties include inulin and polysaccharides.

Antipruritic properties: Chicory tea has antipruritic properties, which can help alleviate the itching associated with ringworm. The active constituents that contribute to chicory's antipruritic properties include essential oils and bitter principles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chicory tea made from the roots has antifungal properties that can help treat ringworm.
Leaves: The leaves of the chicory plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe skin irritation caused by ringworm.
Flowers: Chicory tea made from the flowers can help boost the immune system and speed up the healing process of ringworm.
Seeds: Chicory tea made from the seeds can help relieve itching and inflammation caused by ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Boil one cup of water in a pot for 3-5 minutes.
Add two teaspoons of dried chicory herb to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herbs, then let it cool down.
Drink two to three cups of this tea daily, for several weeks, to help treat ringworm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tea helps with ringworm because it contains high levels of silicon, which is known to boost the body's production of collagen and elastin, two proteins that help to strengthen and repair the skin. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of horsetail also help to soothe irritated skin and combat the fungal infection that causes ringworm. Additionally, horsetail is a natural astringent, helping to dry out the affected area and promote healing. Drinking horsetail tea can also help to boost the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off the infection and speed up the healing process.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Horsetail tea contains antifungal compounds, such as phenolic acids and flavonoids, which help to inhibit the growth of the fungus responsible for ringworm.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Horsetail tea has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm.
Antimicrobial properties: Horsetail tea contains antimicrobial agents, such as silica and tannins, that can help prevent secondary infections and promote healing of the affected area.
Astringent properties: Horsetail tea has astringent properties, which can help to dry out the affected area and reduce the spread of the fungus.
Antioxidant properties: Horsetail tea contains antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help to protect the skin from damage and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The underground stem of the horsetail tea plant contains the highest concentration of beneficial compounds, making it an effective ingredient in medicinal tea for treating ringworm.
Leaves: The leaves of the horsetail tea plant have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tea for treating ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried horsetail herb and place it in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the horsetail herb, allowing it to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Cover the teapot or cup to retain the heat and essential oils.
Strain the tea to remove the horsetail herb.
Drink 2-3 cups of the horsetail tea daily, or use it as a topical treatment by letting it cool and applying it directly to the affected area.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with ringworm because it has antifungal and antimicrobial properties that can combat the fungus causing the infection. The tea is made from the seeds of the fennel plant, which contain compounds like trans-anethole, fenchone, and estragole that inhibit the growth of fungi. Regular consumption of fennel tea can help alleviate the symptoms of ringworm, such as itching, redness, and inflammation. Furthermore, fennel tea can boost the immune system, enabling the body to fight off the infection more effectively. However, it is important to note that fennel tea should be used as a complementary treatment and not a substitute for medical treatment prescribed by a healthcare professional.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Fennel tea contains compounds such as trans-anethole and fenchone, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for ringworm.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel tea can help reduce inflammation and redness associated with ringworm, providing relief from itching and discomfort, thanks to its flavonoid and essential oil content.
Antioxidant properties: Fennel tea is rich in antioxidants like rosmarinic acid and quercetin, which help boost the immune system and promote healing of ringworm-infected skin.
Antipruritic properties: Fennel tea has the ability to alleviate itching, a common symptom of ringworm, due to its anesthetic and anti-inflammatory properties.

Antibacterial properties: Fennel tea can help prevent secondary bacterial infections that may occur due to scratching or broken skin, as it contains compounds like camphene and limonene that inhibit bacterial growth.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of the fennel tea plant contain compounds that have antifungal properties, making them effective in treating ringworm.
Leaves: The leaves of the fennel tea plant are rich in antioxidants, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in ringworm infections.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the fennel tea plant have antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent the spread of ringworm and promote recovery.
Flowers: The flowers of the fennel tea plant contain essential oils that have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in treating ringworm.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of crushed fennel seeds.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the fennel seeds, then let it cool to a comfortable temperature.
Drink 2-3 cups of the fennel tea daily, preferably between meals.
Continue this regimen for 2-3 weeks, or until ringworm symptoms subside.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with ringworm because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to have antifungal properties. Ringworm is a fungal infection, so drinking licorice tea can help to combat the infection and promote healing. The tea can also help to reduce inflammation and itching, which are common symptoms of ringworm. Additionally, licorice tea is a natural remedy, which means that it is a safer alternative to over-the-counter antifungal creams and medications, which can sometimes have unwanted side effects. However, it is important to note that licorice tea should not be used as a sole treatment for ringworm, and it is always best to consult with a healthcare provider for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedy_properties: Anti-fungal: Licorice tea contains compounds such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids that have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for ringworm.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of licorice tea help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm infections.
Immune-boosting: Licorice tea can enhance the immune system, which aids the body in fighting off and recovering from ringworm infections.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in licorice tea helps protect the skin from damage and promotes healing of ringworm-affected areas.
Wound-healing: Licorice tea has been found to have wound-healing properties, which can speed up the recovery process and minimize scarring from ringworm infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antifungal compounds, making them effective in treating ringworm infections.
Leaves: Contain compounds with antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, helpful in alleviating skin irritation caused by ringworm.
Seeds: High in antioxidants and fungicidal properties, aiding in the elimination of ringworm and promoting skin healing.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried licorice root, then reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the licorice root, then let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Drink 1 cup of the licorice tea, 3 times a day, for at least 2 weeks.
Discard any remaining tea after 24 hours and prepare a fresh batch as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's Wort tea helps with ringworm because it contains active compounds such as hypericin and hyperforin, which have been found to have antifungal properties. These compounds can help to inhibit the growth of the fungus that causes ringworm, thereby promoting healing and reducing the severity of symptoms. The tea is made from the leaves and flowers of the St. John's Wort plant, and it can be consumed internally or applied topically to the affected area. Its anti-inflammatory properties can also help to reduce redness, itching, and irritation associated with ringworm. However, it is important to note that St. John's Wort tea should not be used as a sole treatment for ringworm and should be used in conjunction with medical treatment recommended by a healthcare professional.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: St. John's wort tea contains active constituents like hypericin and hyperforin, which exhibit antifungal effects that can help inhibit the growth of the fungus causing ringworm.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, providing relief to the affected area.
Wound healing properties: St. John's wort tea promotes wound healing, and its antimicrobial properties can help prevent secondary infections, supporting the recovery process from ringworm.
Immune system support: The tea can help boost the immune system, enhancing the body's ability to fight off infections and promoting faster recovery from ringworm.
Antioxidant properties: St. John's wort tea contains antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage and support overall skin health, which is essential for managing and preventing ringworm.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of St. John's Wort are rich in antimicrobial compounds, making them effective in treating ringworm infections.
Leaves: The leaves of St. John's Wort contain hypericin and hyperforin, which have antifungal properties that help to alleviate symptoms of ringworm.
Stems: St. John's Wort stems contain active compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce redness and swelling associated with ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried St. John's Wort herb.
Place the herb in a tea infuser or teapot with a built-in strainer.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the herb and let it steep for 10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea before pouring it into a cup.
Drink 3 cups of the St. John's Wort tea daily, between meals, for up to 2 weeks to treat ringworm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with ringworm because it has antifungal properties that can help to combat the fungus causing the infection. The main active compound in lavender, linalool, has been shown to inhibit the growth of various fungi, including those responsible for ringworm. Furthermore, lavender tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe the itching and irritation associated with ringworm. Drinking lavender tea or applying it topically as a compress may provide relief and promote healing of the affected area. However, it is important to note that while lavender tea can be a helpful addition to a treatment plan, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment for ringworm.
remedy_properties: Antifungal property: English lavender tea contains compounds such as linalool and eucalyptol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for ringworm.

Anti-inflammatory property: The tea's ability to reduce inflammation, due to the presence of compounds like camphor and tannins, can help alleviate the redness, itching, and swelling associated with ringworm.
Wound-healing property: English lavender tea promotes wound healing, partly because of its tannins content, which can help reduce the spread of ringworm and speed up recovery.
Antimicrobial property: The tea's antimicrobial properties, attributed to compounds like linalyl acetate, can help prevent secondary infections and further complications in ringworm-affected areas.

Soothing property: The soothing effect of English lavender tea, thanks to its volatile oils, can help provide relief from the discomfort and itchiness caused by ringworm, contributing to overall well-being and recovery.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antifungal compounds, making them effective in treating ringworm.
Leaves: Contain high levels of therapeutic compounds, providing relief from itching and inflammation caused by ringworm.
Stems: Infused in tea, stems provide astringent properties, helping to dry out the affected area and promote healing.
Buds: Packed with antifungal and anti-inflammatory agents, buds aid in reducing redness and swelling while eliminating the fungus.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried English lavender flowers, letting it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
After simmering, strain the tea to remove the lavender flowers, pouring the liquid into a cup.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature, usually around 10-15 minutes.
Soak a clean cloth in the cooled tea, ensuring it is fully saturated.
Apply the soaked cloth directly to the affected area for 15-20 minutes, repeating this process 2-3 times a day.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat ringworm with herbal teas is by using a strong tea made from antifungal herbs such as calendula, Oregon grape, or goldenseal. Steep the herbs in boiling water for 10-15 minutes, then let the tea cool. Apply the tea directly to the affected area using a cotton ball or cloth, allowing it to dry on the skin. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day for several weeks, or until the ringworm has fully healed. Remember to wash your hands thoroughly after each application to prevent spreading the infection. Always consult with a healthcare provider for proper diagnosis and treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ringworm that are treated with herbal teas include candidiasis and other fungal infections. Certain herbal teas, such as those made from chamomile, calendula, and echinacea, have antifungal properties that can help to combat these types of infections. Chamomile tea, for example, contains flavonoids that have been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, a common cause of fungal infections. Similarly, calendula tea has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe skin irritations and promote healing. Echinacea tea, on the other hand, can help to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation, making it a useful remedy for fungal infections. However, it is important to note that while herbal teas can be helpful in treating fungal infections, they should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially in severe or persistent cases.
